data.title: Teck eyes U.S., Canada minerals deals amid China’s export crackdown
data.description:
Teck Resources  (TSX:TECK.B:CA) (NYSE:TECK) was in talks with the U.S. and Canada about providing critical minerals to the countries' key defense industries, a day after China tightened its rare earth export rules. 

The firm said it was in discussions with the two countries over the potential supply of germanium, antimony, and gallium if it can secure minimum prices and purchase guarantees, the Financial Times reported.

China's announcement clarified and expanded sweeping controls announced in April, which had caused massive shortages around the world before a series of deals with Europe and the U.S. resumed shipments. The new announcement, however, makes it clear that licenses will likely be denied to arms makers and some semiconductor companies.

Teck Resources CEO Jonathan Price at the FT Metals and Mining Summit said the firm could produce enough germanium to supply all the needs of North America and potentially of the G7 countries as well. 

Price at the summit added that China has used its dominance in the markets to some extent as a coercive tool in trade relationships.

The firm is currently in the midst of a massive merger. Teck Resources (TSX:TECK.B:CA) (NYSE:TECK) and London-listed miner Anglo American (OTCQX:AAUKF) (OTCQX:NGLOY) had reached an agreement to combine the two companies in a merger of equals to form the Anglo Teck group.

data.title: Trump threatens to pull out of Xi meeting, 'massive' new tariffs on China
data.description:
President Donald Trump said on Friday he now sees "no reason" to meet with Chinese President Xi Jinping and threatened a "massive increase" in tariffs on Chinese goods, in response to what he called the nation's "hostile" export controls on rare earths.

China has been sending letters to many other countries, saying "they want to impose Export Controls on each and every element of production having to do with Rare Earths, and virtually anything else they can think of, even if it’s not manufactured in China," Trump wrote in a post on Truth Social.

He appeared to be caught off guard by China's move. "Our relationship with China over the past six months has been a very good one, thereby making this move on Trade an even more surprising one," Trump wrote. "This was a real surprise, not only to me, but to all the Leaders of the Free World."

The turn of events puts into question Trump's scheduled meeting with Xi.

"I was to meet President Xi in two weeks, at APEC, in South Korea, but now there seems to be no reason to do so," he added.

The U.S. president said a "massive" increase in tariffs is one policy his administration is considering. "There are many other countermeasures that are, likewise, under serious consideration," he said, without providing any examples.

Trump also warned that the retaliatory actions may cause some pain for the U.S., but be worth it in the end. "Ultimately, though potentially painful, it will be a very good thing, in the end, for the U.S.A.," he said.

U.S. stock averages slumped. The Nasdaq dipped 1.8%, the S&P 500 declined 1.5%, and the Dow was off 1.1% in midday trading.

iShares MSCI China A ETF (BATS:CNYA) fell 3.4%, iShares China Large-Cap ETF (NYSEARCA:FXI) slid 3.1%, and SPDR S&P China ETF (NYSEARCA:GXC) dropped 3.5%.

Other relevant tickers include: (MCHI), (YINN), (YANG), (KWEB),  (PGJ).

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Bitcoin breaks below $120K after Trump's 'massive' tariff threat to China
data.description:
Bitcoin's (BTC-USD) price fell below $120K in Friday late morning trading after U.S. President Donald Trump's comments about China sparked renewed concerns over tariffs and spurred a broader selloff across risk assets.

The first-ever cryptocurrency (BTC-USD) fell to as low as $118.6K, before recovering slightly to $119K at 11:57 a.m. ET, down 2.2% in the last 24 hours. Ether (ETH-USD) gapped down 5.8% to $4.11K, Mantle (MANTLE-USD) -12%, Okex (OKB-USD) -4.9%, Pepe (PEPE-USD) -4.9%, Avalanche (AVAX-USD) -4.5% and Cronos (CRO-USD) -4.3%.

The swings come after Trump said China has "become very hostile" sending letters to other countries wanting to "impose export controls." He threated "massive" tariff increases on goods from China, citing export controls that China imposed on rare-earth minerals from that country. Stocks, in turn, reversed earlier gains.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Palm Valley Capital Fund takes new stakes in TFX, RHI, LKQ and AVA
data.description:
Palm Valley Capital Fund increased 2.35%, trailing the 9.11% gain for the S&P SmallCap 600 and 7.99% increase for the Morningstar Small Cap Total Return index during the quarter, .
 
The fund acquired four positions during the third quarter, Teleflex (NYSE:TFX), Robert Half (NYSE:RHI), and LKQ (NASDAQ:LKQ) and initiated position in Avista (NYSE:AVA).
 
The Fund held 73.5% in cash equivalents at the beginning of the quarter and 74.1% at the end. Excluding cash and the impact of fund expenses, the Fund’s equity positions rose by 6.4% over the three months.

data.title: Doximity downgraded at J.P. Morgan on valuation and advertising concerns
data.description:
Shares of Doximity (NYSE:DOCS) traded lower on Friday after J.P. Morgan downgraded the California-based digital platform for medical professionals to Underweight from Neutral, citing concerns over its valuation and headwinds in the pharma advertising space.

Analyst Alexei Gogolev argued that Doximity (NYSE:DOCS) warrants a premium multiple relative to J.P. Morgan’s coverage within the Vertical SaaS universe, given its strong financial profile. However, reaffirming his $62 per share target, the analyst added that its 2026E EV/EBITDA multiple of 36x indicates a sizable premium compared to a median multiple of 23x in the peer group.

Additionally, Gogolev wrote that as a proxy for its revenue growth, Doximity (NYSE:DOCS) has used growth in pharma’s digital marketing targeted at healthcare professionals. The analyst pointed out that DOCS' FY26 outlook suggested a target growth twice the size of Pharma HCP digital marketing growth of 5%-6%.

“The shift to digital advertising has benefited DOCS, but growth remains tied to clinical trial outcomes and regulatory changes,” he wrote, adding that there is a lack of clarity over the company’s growth outlook amid uncertainty over pharma digital advertising trends.

Gogolev noted that other healthcare-related headwinds, such as labor shortages and staff turnover, have become additional challenges to the company’s demand, further worsened by an aging population.

data.title: Santander tapped by U.S. Treasury for Argentine peso intervention - report
data.description:
The U.S. Treasury Department has picked Spainish lender Banco Santander (NYSE:SAN) to serve as its conduit for buying Argentine pesos in a bid to support the South American economy, according to a media report on Friday.

Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said this week the U.S. has finalized a $20B currency swap framework with Argentina, following several days of talk between the two countries' economic teams.

Santander (NYSE:SAN) was selecated for its status as a primary dealer in U.S. Treasury securities, as well as and its strong presence in both New York and Buenos Aires. Primary dealers are approved to trade directly with the Federal Reserve, helping it buy and sell government bonds.

The bank has been a primary dealer to the Fed since its 2021 takeover of broker Amherst Pierpoint Securities.

Ahead of Argentine President Javier Milei's planned visit the Oval Office next week, Bessent said in an X post that "we do not want another failed or China-led state in Latin America. Stabilizing Argentina is America First."

Santander (SAN) did not immediately respond to Seeking Alpha's request for comment.

data.title: Morgan Stanley to open up crypto investing to all its clients - report
data.description:
Morgan Stanley (NYSE:MS) informed its network of financial advisors that crypto investments will be available to all clients in any type of account, including retirement accounts.

As of Oct. 15, advisors will be able to offer crypto funds to any client, a person familiar with the matter told Seeking Alpha.

Previously, investing in the asset class was only open to clients with an aggressive risk tolerance and at least $1.5M in assets, and only allowed the investment in a taxable brokerage account. CNBC reported on the development earlier on Friday, citing people familiar.

Morgan Stanley (NYSE:MS), parent of the E*Trade online brokerage, has built up its wealth and investment management operations in recent years, bringing the unit's client assets to $8.2T.

The bank's global investment committee's recommendation in a recently issued model is a maximum initial allocation to crypto of up to 4%, depending on the investor's goals.

The company will use an automated monitoring process to ensure that clients aren't overly concentrated in the risky asset class, the people said.

Late last month, Bloomberg News reported that Morgan Stanley (NYSE:MS) was partnering with Zerohash, a stablecoin infrastructure platform, to allow E*Trade users to trade in popular crypto tokens starting in H1 2026.

Morgan Stanley (NYSE:MS) stock rose 0.4% in Friday morning trading. In crypto-trading related stocks, Coinbase Global (COIN) fell 0.3%, Robinhood Markets (HOOD) slid 1.6%, Bullish (BLSH) sank 3.8%, and eToro Group (ETOR) rose 0.3%.

In Friday trading, bitcoin (BTC-USD) slipped 0.3% in the past 24 hours to $121.3K, and ether (ETH-USD) rose 0.2% to ~$4.33K.

data.title: Search Minerals announces revised terms for non-brokered private placement financing
data.description:
• Search Minerals (OTCPK:SHCMF) stated that it has amended the terms of its previously announced non-brokered private placement financings for gross proceeds of up to $0.99M.
  • Under the revised terms, the hard dollar private placement price has increased from $0.25 to $0.33 per share.
  • The financing will consist of a non-brokered charity flow-through unit of up to 1.49M units at $0.50 each for gross proceeds of up to $0.74M  and a hard dollar unit of up to 0.75M shares at a price of $0.33 for a gross proceed of $0.25M, each including one flow-through share and half a warrant.
  • Each warrant will be exercisable to acquire one non-flow-through common share of the company at an exercise price of $0.65 for a period of 24 months following the date of issuance.
  • The net proceeds from the share financing will be used by the company for working capital and general corporate purposes.

data.title: CoreWeave in spotlight as insiders cash out after huge run: report
data.description:
• CoreWeave (NASDAQ:CRWV) was in the spotlight on Friday as Bloomberg reported that several insiders sold some of their equity holdings following a huge run in the stock of the artificial intelligence data center company.
  • Among those selling shares were: director Jack Cogen, who sold $477M worth of stock in the third-quarter; and co-founder Brannin McBee, who sold $426M, the news outlet reported, citing data from Washington Service, which tracks insider buying and selling. Both share sells from Cogen and McBee were part of a pre-arrange 10b5-1 stock trading plan. 
  • Others tied to the company also sold stock, including hedge fund Magnetar Financial LLC, which sold approximately $1.9B worth of stock in the quarter. Magnetar is CoreWeave's largest investor. 
  • CoreWeave shares rose nearly 3% in morning trading.

data.title: Core Scientific holders expected to reject CoreWeave deal - report
data.description:
Core Scientific (NASDAQ:CORZ) shareholders are expected to reject CoreWeave's (NASDAQ:CRWV) acquisition when they vote later this month. Shares of Core Scientific rose 5.5%, while CoreWeave gained 3%. 

Core Scientific (NASDAQ:CORZ) holders will vote down the $9 billion acquisition at a shareholder vote on Oct. 30, according to CNBC's David Faber, who cited large holders of the stock as well as activist investors.

The comments come after CoreWeave CEO Michael Intrator said on Tuesday that  there's no plan to readdress the Core Scientific (NASDAQ:CORZ) bid even as some shareholders have come out against the deal. 

“Really under no circumstances will we readdress the bid that we put out,” CoreWeave CEO Intrator said in an interview on Bloomberg Television. “That’s the number. We think it fairly represents the value for them. We think it will be great for the two companies to move forward together.”

Core Scientific holders have publicly come out against the proposed $9 billion deal, saying that it undervalues CORZ. Some investors believe Core Scientific is worth more than the original valuation, and should be bought for a figure closer to $25 a share, or $11.5bn, the Financial Times reported in August.

Under the proposed agreement, Core Scientific (CORZ) shareholders would receive 0.1235 newly issued shares of CoreWeave for each share they hold. Many top shareholders and hedge funds are debating whether to reject the deal altogether, aiming to force improved terms or even to scuttle the transaction if their expectations are not met.
